title: ranunculus bulbosus f. macranthus
intro: Ranunculus bulbosus f. macranthus, also known as the greater cleft buttercup, is a variant of the Ranunculus bulbosus plant, which is a perennial herb belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its alkaloid and glycoside content. The health benefits of Ranunculus bulbosus f. macranthus include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, but caution should be exercised due to its toxic alkaloids and potential skin irritation.
